title: cirsium scabrum
intro: Cirsium scabrum, also known as rough thistle, is a perennial plant native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its distinctive thistle-like leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin issues, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Cirsium scabrum include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and acting as a natural antacid, while its herbal preparations include teas, infusions, and topical creams or ointments. In addition to its medicinal properties, Cirsium scabrum is also used as a food source, with its flowers and leaves being edible and rich in vitamins and minerals.
